rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner 255 Berry Homeowners Association'S Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To Cc&R'S And/Or In The Alternative To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To CC&R'S And/Or In The Alternative To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments. Denied. Although the requirements set forth in Civ. Code sec. 4275 are met, Petitioner has not demonstrated that "because of voter apathy or other reasons, important amendments cannot be approved by the normal procedures¿" (Fourth La Costa Condominium Owners Ass'n v. Seith (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 563, 570.) The evidence establishes that an amendment was approved by a super-majority of voters, as required by the CC&Rs, in the very same election. (Declaration of Judith Taylor, Ex. 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THEODORE F SCHROEDER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Non-Parties' Amended Petition To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 4: Non-Parties PINTEREST, INC., BEN SILBERMANN, MICHELE DIGIACOMO, EVAN SHARP, PAUL SCIARRA, and JUSTIN EDMUND'S Hearing On Amended Petition To Quash. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLY CARPER et al VS. ADKNOWLEDGE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Attendance At Depositions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT ADKNOWLEDGE INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Attendance At Depositions And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant's motion is DENIED in its entirety. The motion to compel the plaintiffs' attendance at deposition is moot as the depositions took place. The plaintiffs' objections to the facially defective original deposition notices was validly served pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.410(a). The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL JAYME et al VS. SETON MEDICAL CENTER, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS, JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs still do not cite any authority for the proposition that a medical device manufacturer can be held strictly liable to a patient for not training the physician on how to use a device. Additionally, Plaintiffs' opposition does not address or distinguish the holding in Carlin v. Superior Court (1996) 13 Cal.4th 1104, 1116, which found that the duty of a medical manufacturer to warn runs to the doctor, not to the patient. Further, in Plenger v. Alza Corp. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 349, 362, the court states that "[w]e are aware of no authority which requires a manufacturer to warn of a risk which is readily known and apparent to the consumer, in this case the physician." Whether training is required by a particular surgeon prior to using a new product during surgery should be apparent to that surgeon and does not require the manufacturer or its sales representative to provide a specific warning about training. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact Plaintiffs have had multiple opportunities to amend and Plaintiffs assert no facts in their opposition about how they could amend to state a viable cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KATHIE LUSTIG VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF KATHIE LUSTIG'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains without leave to amend as to defense 37 (reservation of rights to raise new defenses). This is not a cognizable defense. The Court overrules as to defense 3 (statute of limitations) as it complies with CCP § 458 because Defendant highlights the specific statute that allegedly time bars the claims. The Court also overrules as to defense 7 (comparative negligence) because it alleges facts that, if true, would entitle Defendant to this defense. The Court sustains with 20 days leave to amend as to the remaining defenses challenged by the Demurrer. They do not meet the pleading requirement of FPI Development, Inc. v. Al Nahashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367. They are completely conclusory and Defendant must allege ultimate facts to support the defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PASTRY COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. JOEL K. BELWAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 15, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS JOEL BELWAY, THE LAW OFFICE OF JOEL K. BELWAY PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the negligence per se "cause of action" is sustained without leave to amend. Negligence per se is an evidentiary doctrine, not a stand alone cause of action. See Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1285. The demurrer to the equitable indemnity cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. It is erroneous to characterize Mr. Adler and defendant as joint tortfeasors. The $165,000 payment is a component of plaintiff's claimed damages for plaintiff's malpractice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
